title: Intersight HCL Compliance Status
spl:
index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:compute" object_type="cond.HclStatus"
| stats latest(status) as hcl_status latest(reason) as reason by server_name, model
| where hcl_status!="Validated"
| table server_name, model, hcl_status, reason
description: Hardware Compatibility List compliance ensures OS, driver, and firmware combinations are Cisco-validated. Non-HCL configurations risk unpredictable failures and void support entitlements.
value: Hardware Compatibility List compliance ensures OS, driver, and firmware combinations are Cisco-validated. Non-HCL configurations risk unpredictable failures and void support entitlements.
implementation: Poll Intersight HCL status via the add-on. Alert when servers move out of Validated status. Correlate with planned OS or driver upgrades.
visualization: Table (non-validated servers), Pie chart (HCL status distribution), Single value (% validated).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Intersight Add-on`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cisco:intersight:compute` (HCL status fields).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll Intersight HCL status via the add-on. Alert when servers move out of Validated status. Correlate with planned OS or driver upgrades.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:compute" object_type="cond.HclStatus"
| stats latest(status) as hcl_status latest(reason) as reason by server_name, model
| where hcl_status!="Validated"
| table server_name, model, hcl_status, reason
```

Understanding this SPL

**Intersight HCL Compliance Status** — Hardware Compatibility List compliance ensures OS, driver, and firmware combinations are Cisco-validated. Non-HCL configurations risk unpredictable failures and void support entitlements.

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:intersight:compute` (HCL status fields). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Intersight Add-on`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_intersight; **sourcetype**: cisco:intersight:compute. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_intersight, sourcetype="cisco:intersight:compute". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by server_name, model** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where hcl_status!="Validated"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Intersight HCL Compliance Status**): table server_name, model, hcl_status, reason


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco Intersight (Alarms, audit, and inventory) for the same object types and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (non-validated servers), Pie chart (HCL status distribution), Single value (% validated).
grandmaExplanation: Hardware Compatibility List compliance ensures OS, driver, and firmware combinations are Cisco-validated — so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
